user.title: Connect to your SX Evolution's CAN bus
user.description: Select your connection method: socketcand daemon or USB adapter.

socketcand.title: Connect via socketcand
socketcand.description: Home Assistant connects to the DOBISS SX Evolution through a socketcand daemon running near the bus.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the machine running socketcand. For example: 192.168.1.10 or homeserver.local.
data_description.port: TCP port socketcand is listening on. The default for this integration is 29536.
data_description.interface: Name of the CAN interface on the socketcand host. The default for this integration is can0.
data_description.master_device: Serial port of the Max200 master module (SX-kabel). Optional, enables clock sync and module import.
data_description.max200_host: Hostname or IP address of the Max200 master module. Optional, enables clock sync and module import.

reauth_socketcand.title: Reconnect to socketcand
reauth_socketcand.description: Home Assistant lost the connection. Confirm or update the socketcand details below.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the machine running socketcand.
data_description.port: TCP port socketcand is listening on (default 29536).
data_description.interface: Name of the CAN interface on the socketcand host (typically can0).
data_description.master_device: Serial port of the Max200 master module (SX-kabel). Optional, enables clock sync and module import.
data_description.max200_host: Hostname or IP address of the Max200 master module. Optional, enables clock sync and module import.

reauth_usb.title: Reconnect USB CAN adapter
reauth_usb.description: Home Assistant lost the connection. Confirm or update the USB CAN adapter details below.

data_description.master_device: Serial port of the Max200 master module (SX-kabel). Optional, enables clock sync and module import.
data_description.max200_host: Hostname or IP address of the Max200 master module. Optional, enables clock sync and module import.

usb_manual.title: Connect via USB CAN adapter
usb_manual.description: Connect directly to a USB CAN adapter like Canable, Lawicel, or other compatible devices.

data_description.device: Path to the serial device. On Linux: /dev/ttyACM0, /dev/ttyUSB0. On Windows: COM3, COM4, etc.
data_description.master_device: Serial port of the Max200 master module (SX-kabel). Optional, enables clock sync and module import.
data_description.max200_host: Hostname or IP address of the Max200 master module. Optional, enables clock sync and module import.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure connection
reconfigure.description: Update how Home Assistant connects to your DOBISS SX Evolution CAN bus.

data_description.connection_type: How Home Assistant connects to the DOBISS CAN bus: socketcand daemon or USB adapter.

reconfigure_socketcand.title: Reconfigure socketcand connection
reconfigure_socketcand.description: Update the socketcand connection details for the DOBISS SX Evolution CAN bus.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the machine running socketcand.
data_description.port: TCP port socketcand is listening on (default 29536).
data_description.interface: Name of the CAN interface on the socketcand host (typically can0).
data_description.master_device: Serial port of the Max200 master module (SX-kabel). Optional, enables clock sync and module import.
data_description.max200_host: Hostname or IP address of the Max200 master module. Optional, enables clock sync and module import.

reconfigure_usb.title: Reconfigure USB connection
reconfigure_usb.description: Update the USB CAN adapter connection details for the DOBISS SX Evolution CAN bus.

data_description.master_device: Serial port of the Max200 master module (SX-kabel). Optional, enables clock sync and module import.
data_description.max200_host: Hostname or IP address of the Max200 master module. Optional, enables clock sync and module import.

error.cannot_connect: Couldn't connect to the CAN bus. Check your connection settings and that the device/daemon is available.
error.unknown: Unexpected error. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.
error.same_output: Up and down outputs must be different.
error.duplicate_output: That output is already in use by another light, shutter, or switch on this module.

abort.already_configured: This DOBISS connection is already set up in Home Assistant.
abort.reauth_successful: Reconnected to the DOBISS CAN bus.
abort.no_outputs_to_remove: This module doesn't have any outputs to remove yet.

user.title: Import modules from Max200
user.description: Download module configuration and output names from the Max200 controller. Modules that already exist will be skipped. All imported outputs default to type light on a non-dimmable module. You can adjust the type and dimmer setting afterwards.

abort.import_successful: Imported {count} new module(s) from the Max200.
abort.import_failed: Could not connect to the Max200. Check the serial port or TCP host and that the controller is powered on.
abort.no_new_modules: All modules from the Max200 are already configured.
abort.no_max200_connection: No Max200 connection configured. Set a TCP host or serial port in the connection settings.

user.title: Add a DOBISS mood
user.description: Enter the mood number as configured in MaxTool. You can find the number in the Sferen tab.

data_description.mood_number: The mood number from MaxTool (0-99). Each number can only be used once.
data_description.name: Optional. A descriptive name like "All lights off" or "Coming home".

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure mood
reconfigure.description: Update the friendly name of this mood. The mood number cannot be changed after creation.

data_description.name: A descriptive name for this mood.

error.mood_number_out_of_range: Mood number must be between 0 and 99.
error.mood_already_exists: A mood with that number already exists.

user.title: Add a DOBISS module
user.description: Each physical DOBISS module gets its own entry here. Once added, you'll be able to attach lights and shutters to it.

data_description.module: The letter assigned to the DOBISS module (A, B, C, ...). Each letter can only be used once.
data_description.name: Optional. Use a name like "Kitchen lights" or "Ground floor" instead of the default "Module A".
data_description.dimmable: Turn this on if the physical module is a dimmer. Every light on it will then expose a brightness slider. Relay modules should leave this off.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure module
reconfigure.description: What would you like to do with this module?

add_light.title: Add a light
add_light.description: Pick the output this light is wired to. Whether it dims or just toggles is determined by the module's dimmer setting.

data_description.output: The output number on the module this light is wired to (1, 2, 3, ...). Each output can only be used by one light, shutter, or switch.
data_description.name: Optional. The name Home Assistant will show, for example "Kitchen ceiling" or "Reading lamp".

add_shutter.title: Add a shutter
add_shutter.description: Shutters use two outputs on the same module: one drives the motor up and the other drives it down.

data_description.up_output: The output number wired to the motor's UP direction.
data_description.down_output: The output number wired to the motor's DOWN direction. Must differ from the up output.
data_description.name: Optional. The name Home Assistant will show, for example "Living room blind" or "Bedroom screen".

add_switch.title: Add a switch
add_switch.description: Pick the output this switch is wired to. Use this for generic on/off relay outputs that are not lights or shutters.

data_description.output: The output number on the module this switch is wired to (1, 2, 3, ...). Each output can only be used by one light, shutter, or switch.
data_description.name: Optional. The name Home Assistant will show for this switch.

remove_output.title: Remove an output
remove_output.description: Pick the light, shutter, or switch you want to remove from this module. Its Home Assistant entity will disappear on the next reload.

data_description.output: Lists every output currently configured on this module.

edit_output.title: Change output type
edit_output.description: Pick the output you want to change.

data_description.output: Lists every output currently configured on this module.

edit_output_type.title: Change output type
edit_output_type.description: Pick the new type for this output.

data_description.type: The entity type: light, shutter, or switch.

edit_output_down.title: Shutter outputs
edit_output_down.description: The selected output drives the motor up. Enter the output wired to the motor's down direction.

data_description.down_output: The output number wired to the motor's DOWN direction. Must differ from the up output.

edit_module.title: Edit module settings
edit_module.description: Update this module's letter, name, or dimmer setting. Toggling the dimmer flag applies to every light already on this module. They will switch between brightness-capable and on/off after the next reload.

data_description.module: The letter assigned to the DOBISS module (A, B, C, ...). Each letter can only be used once.
data_description.name: Optional. Use a name like "Kitchen lights" or "Ground floor" instead of the default "Module A".
data_description.dimmable: Turn this on if the physical module is a dimmer. Applies to every light on this module.

error.invalid_module: Use a single letter from A to Z.
error.module_already_exists: You already have a module with that letter.
error.invalid_output: Output number must be 1 or higher.
error.duplicate_output: Another light, shutter, or switch on this module is already using that output.
error.same_output: Up and down need to be different outputs.
error.no_outputs_to_remove: This module doesn't have any outputs to remove yet.

abort.no_outputs_to_remove: This module doesn't have any outputs to remove yet.
abort.no_outputs_to_edit: This module doesn't have any outputs to edit yet.

refresh.name: Refresh state
refresh.description: Asks DOBISS to broadcast the current state of every output. Useful when Home Assistant's cached state is out of sync.

sync_clock.name: Sync clock
sync_clock.description: Sends the current Home Assistant time to the Max200 controller now, instead of waiting for the periodic sync. Requires a Max200 serial port or TCP host to be configured on the integration. Over TCP the command is sent without confirmation, over serial a failure is reported.

cannot_send.message: Couldn't send the command to DOBISS. Check that socketcand and the CAN interface are still up.

no_clock_link.message: No Max200 serial port or TCP host is configured on any DOBISS entry, there is nothing to sync the clock to.

clock_sync_failed.message: Could not deliver the clock command to the Max200 over serial. Check the cable and the configured serial port.

cannot_connect.title: DOBISS CAN bus connection lost
cannot_connect.description: Home Assistant can no longer connect to the DOBISS CAN bus. Check that your connection device/daemon is available and your network/USB connection is healthy. Use **Reconfigure** on the DOBISS SX Evolution integration to update the connection settings if they have changed.
